zh.lifeSummary: Mrs Randle de Sylvester於大約1145年出生在英國爾斯托雷頓。她於1170年之前與Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral在英國結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Mrs Randle de Sylvester于大约1145年出生在英国爾斯托雷頓。她于1170年之前与Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral在英国结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Sylvester
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and German: from the Middle English and Latin personal name Silvester ‘dweller in the forest’ (a derivative of silva ‘wood’). The name was borne by three popes, including a contemporary of Constantine the Great, and seems to have been first used in England by clerics.

variants.name: Silvestre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Manuel, Fernando, Juan, Miguel, Carlos, Ramon, Raul, Efren, Josefina, Mario, Pedro.  Portuguese Joao, Joaquim.
Galician and Spanish: habitational name from any of the places called Silvestre in Pontevedra and Badajoz.
Catalan, French (Occitan), and Spanish: from the personal name Silvestre (see Sylvester ).

variants.name: Selwyn
meanings: English (Gloucestershire):
from the Middle English personal name Selewin(e) (Old English Selewine, perhaps from sele ‘hall’ or sǣl ‘happiness, prosperity’ + wine ‘friend’).

of Norman origin, perhaps from the Middle English and Old French personal name Selvein, Silvein (from Latin Silvanus, an adjective from silva ‘woodland’), with vocalization of /v/ to /w/, possibly through influence of the name above. In Anglo-Norman usage both Selvein and Silvester were sometimes Latinized as Salvagius ‘wild, savage’ (see Sylvester and Savage ), though whether the vernacular surnames were ever interchangeable is a question for further research.

variants.name: West
meanings: English and German: from Middle English, Middle High German west ‘west’, hence a topographic name for someone who lived to the west of a settlement, or a habitational name for someone who had migrated from further west.
Americanized form of Finnish Vesterinen: from the personal name Vesteri (a short form of Sylvester ) + the surname suffix -nen.

History: This name was brought to North America independently by many bearers in the 17th and 18th centuries. Thomas West, 12th Baron De La Warre, was captain general of Virginia in 1610–11. The state of DE is named for him. One of the earliest permanent settlers was Francis West (1606–92), who came to Duxbury, MA, from Salisbury, Wiltshire, England, in or before 1638.

variants.name: Sylvestre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Armand, Normand, Pierre, Adelard, Aime, Alphonse, Carmelle, Donat, Gilles, Jean Claude, Marcel.
French and West Indian (mainly Haiti): from the French personal name Sylvestre (see Sylvester ).
History: Nicolas Sylvestre dit Champagne from Pont-sur-Seine in Aube, France, married Barbe Neveu in Quebec City, QC, in 1667.

variants.name: Sule
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Aparna, Dileep, Geeta, Harsh, Pushkar, Sangeeta, Vikram.  Hungarian Mihaly, Sandor.
Indian (Maharashtra; pronounced as two syllables): Maratha name, from Marathi suḷa ‘pointed tooth’, from Sanskrit šūla ‘spike, spear’.
Hungarian: from a pet form of the personal name Szilveszter (see Sylvester ).

variants.name: Silvester
meanings: English and German: variant of   Sylvester  .

variants.name: Silvestro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenic, Angelo, Sal, Caesar, Agostino, Antonio, Camillo, Carmelo, Dino, Ennio, Gaetano, Giovanni.
Italian: from the personal name Silvestro, Latin Silvester (see Sylvester ).

variants.name: Sylvest
meanings: Danish: from a personal name, a shortened form of Latin Silvester (see Sylvester ).

nameMeanings.name: Randle
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mainly U.S.: medieval vernacular form of   Randolf .  This was in common use as a given name into the 17th century and gave rise to a surname. In modern use the given name is often a transferred use of this surname.

variants.name: Randell
meanings: Mainly U.S.: medieval vernacular form of   Randolf .  This was in common use as a given name into the 17th century and gave rise to a surname. In modern use the given name is often a transferred use of this surname.

variants.name: Randal
meanings: Mainly U.S.: medieval vernacular form of   Randolf .  This was in common use as a given name into the 17th century and gave rise to a surname. In modern use the given name is often a transferred use of this surname.

variants.name: Randel
meanings: Mainly U.S.: medieval vernacular form of   Randolf .  This was in common use as a given name into the 17th century and gave rise to a surname. In modern use the given name is often a transferred use of this surname.

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Mrs Randle de Sylvester was born about 1145, in Storeton, Cheshire, England. She married Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral before 1170, in England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter.

fr.lifeSummary: Mrs Randle de Sylvester est née environ 1145, à Storeton, Cheshire, Angleterre. Elle a épousé Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral avant 1170, à Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille.

de.lifeSummary: Mrs Randle de Sylvester wurde um 1145 in Storeton, Cheshire, England geboren. Sie hat vor 1170 in England Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Mrs Randle de Sylvester è nata il circa 1145 nel Storeton, Cheshire, England. Lei sposato Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral prima del 1170, nel England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia.

ja.Storeton, Cheshire, England: イギリスチェシュアイアストーレトン
ja.lifeSummary: Mrs Randle de Sylvesterは約1145年にイギリスチェシュアイアストーレトンで生まれた。1170年以前にイギリスでRanulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirralと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。

ko.lifeSummary: Mrs Randle de Sylvester(은)는 대략 1145년 영국 체실르 스토레턴에서 태어났다. 그녀는 1170년 이전 영국에서 Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Mrs Randle de Sylvester nasceu aproximadamente 1145, em Storeton, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Ela casou-se com Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral antes 1170, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha.

ru.lifeSummary: Mrs Randle de Sylvester родилась около 1145 г. в местности сторетон, чешир, Англия. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral перед 1170 г. в местности Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.

es.lifeSummary: Mrs Randle de Sylvester nació aproximadamente 1145, en Storeton, Cheshire, Inglaterra. Se casó con Ranulph FitzAlan de Sylvester, of the Wirral antes de 1170, en Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija.
